symptoms.symptom_name: Sensation of a skipped beat followed by a single forceful thud

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A skipped beat with a compensatory pause and a forceful post-ectopic beat is the classic subjective hallmark of ventricular ectopy.
Palpitations are non-specific; an isolated jolt is less typical of AF than a chaotic sustained flutter.

symptoms.symptom_name: Increased awareness of the heartbeat lying quietly at night

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ectopy is characteristically noticed at rest/at night when competing stimuli are low and vagal tone shifts.

symptoms.symptom_name: Brief light-headedness with the forceful beat, no true syncope

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Transient pre-syncope can accompany the post-ectopic pause and is usually benign.
Light-headedness with palpitations is the feature that keeps a haemodynamically significant arrhythmia on the list until an ECG excludes it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Regular underlying rhythm between beats (not sustained/chaotic/racing)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A steady regular baseline interrupted by isolated beats is unlike the irregularly irregular, sustained rhythm of AF.
Isolated prematures on a regular sinus baseline is the expected pattern of benign ectopy.

symptoms.symptom_name: High caffeine intake, fatigue, sleep deprivation and stress

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sympathetic drive from caffeine, fatigue and stress is a well-recognised precipitant of benign ectopy and a modifiable target.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of chest pain / exertional symptoms / resting dyspnoea

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The absence of ischaemic and heart-failure features lowers, but does not eliminate, the probability of an ischaemic driver.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe acute health anxiety / fear the heart is stopping

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Marked anxiety amplifies symptom perception; it is a co-driver and a management target, not a safe sole explanation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Premature beat with a compensatory pause on pulse palpation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A regular pulse interrupted by a premature beat and compensatory pause is the classic examination sign — clinician-only, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Premature loud heart sound, pause, then forceful post-ectopic beat on auscultation; no murmur/gallop/raised JVP

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Auscultatory confirmation of ectopy with no signs of structural disease or heart failure — clinician-only, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Broad, unassociated (AV-dissociated) QRS complexes on 12-lead ECG

investigation_details.expected_result: Wide-complex premature ventricular beats with full compensatory pause; no acute ischaemic changes; PR/rhythm otherwise normal
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without an ECG, ventricular ectopy cannot be confirmed and AF, ischaemia and AV block cannot be excluded.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The broad unassociated QRS on the tracing is the confirmatory feature — requires equipment the patient does not have, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal blood pressure and heart rate, afebrile, normal SpO2 (clinician-measured)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Stable haemodynamics support a benign process — but this is a clinician measurement; the AI Doctor has no device reading and cannot assume it.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Isolated forceful beats on a regular baseline, worse at rest, with a clear caffeine/fatigue trigger — the recognisable benign-ectopy constellation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line and essential: confirms ventricular ectopy and excludes AF, acute ischaemia and AV block — the telehealth AI cannot obtain it and must arrange it urgently.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum electrolytes (potassium, magnesium) and thyroid function
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Screens for hypokalaemia/hypomagnesaemia and thyrotoxicosis as reversible ectopy drivers.
Quantifies ectopic burden and captures symptom-rhythm correlation if symptoms persist despite lifestyle change — not a first-visit test.
Not indicated without ischaemic features; ordering routinely here would be an over-investigation of a well, pain-free patient (recommend only if chest pain or ECG ischaemia emerges).
